mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profits and stock prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 19681 signals a strengthening consumer market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 220 thousand signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining initial jobless claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures above 680591 signals a strengthening consumer market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real personal consumption expenditures are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
